title: Logical Operators 🔗
description: Combine multiple conditions using AND, OR, and NOT.

slides.title: Combining Conditions 🧩
slides.content:
Sometimes you want an action to happen only if MULTIPLE things are true at the same time.

Ruby has three **Logical Operators** that allow you to chain conditions together:

1. `&&` (AND)
2. `||` (OR)
3. `!` (NOT)

slides.title: The AND Operator &&
slides.content:
The double ampersand `&&` ensures that BOTH conditions are strictly `true` before running.

If even one condition is false, the whole thing evaluates to false.

```ruby
if age > 18 && has_ticket == true
  puts "Enjoy the movie!"
end
```

quizData.question: What does `true && false` evaluate to?
quizData.explanation: Because `&&` requires EVERYTHING to be true, returning false on one side ruins the entire statement.

slides.title: The OR Operator ||
slides.content:
The double pipe `||` (located above the Enter key) implies OR.

It runs the block if **AT LEAST ONE** condition is `true`. It doesn't care if the other one is false.

```ruby
if day == "Saturday" || day == "Sunday"
  puts "It is the weekend!"
end
```

quizData.question: What does `true || false` evaluate to?
quizData.explanation: Because `||` only requires ONE side to be true, seeing the first `true` makes the whole statement evaluate to true.

slides.title: The NOT Operator !
slides.content:
The exclamation mark `!` is known as the **Bang** or **NOT** operator. It flips a boolean to its exact opposite!

`!true` becomes `false`
`!false` becomes `true`

slides.title: Flipping Booleans
slides.content: See how the `!` operator flips the truth around.
slides.codeSnippet:
is_raining = false

if !is_raining
  puts "Let's go to the park!"
end

slides.title: Short Circuiting ⚡
slides.content: Ruby is smart. If it evaluates the left side of `||` and sees it's `true`, it immediately stops reading. It doesn't even bother checking the right side because it knows the entire statement will be true!
